story.tasks: - Task 1: Install and configure nestjs-i18n package (AC: 7.1.1, 7.1.3) - Task 2: Create English translation files (AC: 7.1.2) - Task 3: Create Turkish translation files (AC: 7.1.2) - Task 4: Test translation service integration (AC: 7.1.4, 7.1.5, 7.1.6) - Task 5: Integration testing with existing modules (AC: 7.1.5)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
AC-7.1.1: nestjs-i18n package installed and configured in I18nModule - Package: nestjs-i18n v10.4.0 installed - I18nModule configured in AppModule - Default language: EN - Fallback language: EN
AC-7.1.2: Translation files created for TR and EN languages - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/en/common.json - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/en/auth.json - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/en/users.json - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/tr/common.json - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/tr/auth.json - Created: src/modules/i18n/translations/tr/users.json
AC-7.1.3: I18nModule configured with language detection - Language detection via Accept-Language header - Default language: EN (if header missing) - Fallback language: EN (if translation key missing)
AC-7.1.4: Translation usage works in services - Service can inject I18nService - Translation lookup works: this.i18n.t('auth.LOGIN_SUCCESS')
AC-7.1.5: Error messages use i18n translations - Exception thrown with translated message - Error responses include translated messages - Language selection based on Accept-Language header
AC-7.1.6: Dynamic variables work in translations - Translation with variable replacement: this.i18n.t('auth.OTP_SENT', { args: { phone: '...' } }) - Variables replaced correctly using {{variable}} syntax

docs.doc: Complete technical specification for i18n setup including nestjs-i18n configuration, translation file structure (TR/EN for common, auth, users modules), Accept-Language header detection, variable replacement mechanism ({{variable}} syntax), and graceful degradation patterns.

Translation file structure with JSON format for EN and TR languages. Defines core translation keys: SUCCESS, ERROR, NOT_FOUND, UNAUTHORIZED, VALIDATION_ERROR, LOGIN_SUCCESS, LOGIN_FAILED, OTP_SENT (with {{phone}} variable), USER_CREATED, USER_NOT_FOUND.
I18nService interface with t() method for translation lookup, variable replacement via args parameter, and language override support. Usage examples for services and exception handling.
Translation lookup performance: < 1ms per key (in-memory cache). Translation files loaded once at application startup. Memory usage: < 10MB for all translation files (TR + EN).

Graceful degradation: Missing translation key returns key itself (not an error). Invalid Accept-Language header falls back to EN. Translation file load error causes application to fail at startup (fail-fast).

Unit test coverage target: 90%+. Test translation lookup, missing keys (graceful degradation), variable replacement, language override, Accept-Language header detection. Integration tests for AuthService and UsersService error messages.

Epic-level story breakdown: Multi-language support with nestjs-i18n. Translation files for EN/TR in common.json, auth.json, users.json namespaces. Translation key format: namespace.KEY (e.g., auth.LOGIN_SUCCESS). Dynamic variables using {{variableName}} syntax.
I18n module is part of Cross-Cutting Concerns layer, providing shared infrastructure for all feature modules. Global module pattern allows all services to inject I18nService for translated messages.

file.reason: Root module where I18nModule needs to be imported and configured. Currently imports ConfigModule, PrismaModule, AuthModule, UsersModule, FilesModule, HealthModule. I18nModule configuration will be added to imports array with forRoot() method, AcceptLanguageResolver, and translations path configuration.

Auth service that will use I18nService for translated error messages. Currently throws ConflictException, BadRequestException, UnauthorizedException with hardcoded English messages (e.g., 'Phone number already exists', 'Password is required for admin role'). These will be replaced with i18n.t() calls.

Users service that will use I18nService for translated error messages. Currently throws NotFoundException with hardcoded message 'User not found'. Will be updated to use i18n.t('users.NOT_FOUND') for language-specific error messages.
Auth module structure for reference. Shows existing module pattern with providers and exports. I18n module will follow similar pattern with I18nModule.forRoot() in AppModule imports.
Users module structure for reference. Shows existing module pattern with controllers, providers, and imports. Services in this module will inject I18nService once i18n module is configured.

package.name: @nestjs/common
package.reason: Core NestJS framework, required for Injectable, Module decorators

package.name: @nestjs/testing
package.reason: Testing utilities for NestJS modules and services

package.name: jest
package.reason: Testing framework for unit and integration tests

story-context.constraints: - I18n Configuration Pattern: Use nestjs-i18n with AcceptLanguageResolver for language detection via Accept-Language HTTP header - Translation Key Naming: Format must be namespace.KEY (e.g., auth.LOGIN_SUCCESS, users.NOT_FOUND) with UPPER_SNAKE_CASE - Translation File Structure: JSON files organized by namespace (common.json, auth.json, users.json) under translations/en/ and translations/tr/ - Variable Replacement: Use {{variableName}} placeholder syntax, pass replacement values via args: { args: { phone: '+90...' } } - Graceful Degradation: Missing translation key must return key itself (not throw error). Invalid Accept-Language header must fallback to default (EN) - Performance Requirement: Translation lookup < 1ms per key (in-memory cache). Files loaded once at startup - Module Integration: I18nModule must be configured as global module in AppModule using I18nModule.forRoot() - Service Pattern: All services can inject I18nService via constructor for translation lookups - Error Message Pattern: Replace all hardcoded error messages with i18n.t() calls (e.g., throw new NotFoundException(this.i18n.t('users.NOT_FOUND'))) - Testing Standards: Unit test coverage target 90%+. Test translation lookup, missing keys, variable replacement, language override, Accept-Language header - File Organization: Create src/modules/i18n/translations/{lang}/{namespace}.json structure before implementing I18nModule - Fail-Fast Principle: Translation file load errors must cause application to fail at startup (not runtime)

interface.name: I18nService.t()
I18nModule.forRoot()
Translation File Format
Exception with i18n

tests.standards: Testing framework: Jest (v30.0.0) with ts-jest for TypeScript support. Unit tests placed in __test__/ directories within modules. Integration tests verify Accept-Language header detection. E2E tests validate translated error messages in API responses. Coverage target: 90%+ for i18n service. Test file naming: *.spec.ts for unit tests.
tests.locations: - src/modules/i18n/__test__/i18n.service.spec.ts (unit tests for I18nService) - src/modules/auth/auth.service.spec.ts (integration tests for i18n in AuthService) - src/modules/users/services/users.service.spec.ts (integration tests for i18n in UsersService) - test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts (E2E tests for Accept-Language header detection)
tests.ideas:
AC-7.1.1, AC-7.1.3: Test I18nModule configuration - Verify I18nModule is imported in AppModule - Test default language is 'en' - Test fallback language is 'en' - Verify AcceptLanguageResolver is configured
AC-7.1.2: Test translation files exist - Verify en/common.json exists with required keys (SUCCESS, ERROR, NOT_FOUND, etc.) - Verify en/auth.json exists with required keys (LOGIN_SUCCESS, OTP_SENT, etc.) - Verify en/users.json exists with required keys (USER_CREATED, USER_NOT_FOUND, etc.) - Verify tr/common.json exists with Turkish translations - Verify tr/auth.json exists with Turkish translations - Verify tr/users.json exists with Turkish translations
AC-7.1.4: Test translation service integration (Unit) - Test I18nService.t() returns correct string for existing key - Test I18nService.t() returns key itself if translation missing (graceful degradation) - Test I18nService.t() with language override (force 'tr' even if header says 'en') - Mock I18nService in service tests
AC-7.1.5: Test error messages with i18n (Integration) - Test AuthService throws exception with translated message - Test UsersService throws exception with translated message - Test error response includes correct language based on Accept-Language header - E2E: Request with Accept-Language: en returns English error message - E2E: Request with Accept-Language: tr returns Turkish error message
AC-7.1.6: Test dynamic variables (Unit) - Test variable replacement: {{phone}} replaced with actual phone number - Test multiple variables: {{firstName}} {{lastName}} both replaced - Test variable replacement in different languages (EN and TR) - Test missing variable (should gracefully handle undefined args)
